title: Pyrrosia intermedia
taxonomy: Pyrrosia intermedia is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Pyrrosia intermedia is a member of the genus Pyrrosia, which consists of several species of terrestrial ferns. The genus Pyrrosia is often included in the subfamily Pyrrosioideae within the family Polypodiaceae. Pyrrosia intermedia is native to tropical Asia, where it can be found growing in shaded areas and forests.

intro: Pyrrosia intermedia, also known as the Intermediate Polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is prized for its potential health benefits, including the ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for cardiovascular and anti-aging purposes. The bioactive constituents of Pyrrosia intermedia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pyrrosia intermedia is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including hypertension, arthritis, and digestive issues.
